ingredients: | Chepa shutki (Bengali dried fermented fish) | 6–8 ea. |  | About 100 grams |
procedure: Clean and wash the fish. If you want, you can remove the scales and extra thorns.
Fry the flat dried fish in a dry pan over low heat until the aroma comes out. Some people fry it with a little oil, but the taste is stronger when fried dry. Remove from the heat, and let cool.
Grind the fish well in a mortar or bowl.
Grind the chiles, garlic, and salt together.
Lightly crush the onion with a fork to release the juice.
Combine all the ingredients together, and serve.
